title: Resource Inventory, Delivery Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Boundaries · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds bounded resource and performance evidence report from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded resource and performance evidence report while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Inventory document, script, style, image, font, media, preload, module, and third-party references with initiator and scope evidence.
Separate encoded transfer, decoded body, in-memory, intrinsic media, and rendered dimensions.
Record cache headers, validators, redirects, protocol, compression, connection context, and cold or warm assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Graph
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 8962 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile resource graphs, size dimensions, cache context, budgets, and static-versus-user performance claims; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory document, script, style, image, font, media, preload, module, and third-party references with initiator and scope evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory document, script, style, image, font, media, preload, module, and third-party references with initiator and scope evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Graph, keep Resource Graph connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Resource Graph preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
The HTML page byte count represents total page weight. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.
